title: CENTER FOR MEDIATION AND DIALOGUE - 211info
text: CITY OF BEAVERTON CENTER FOR MEDIATION AND DIALOGUE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential mediation, facilitation, and conflict resolution education. Types of mediation include: Community Mediation: Conflicts involving noise, fences, parking, vandalism, animal complaints, property damage or maintenance, harassment, minor assaults, and conflicts over money and personal property. Foreclosure Avoidance Mediation: Utilizes specially trained facilitators to conduct face-to-face resolution conferences between borrowers and lenders to avoid home foreclosure. Housing Mediation: For tenants, landlords/housing providers wishing to discuss issues related to rent, repairs, communication, or any other issues or concerns related to the living arrangement. Manufactured Home (MFH) Mediation: Assists homeowners, residents, managers, and park owners to resolve conflicts associated with the parks and living situations. Home Sharing Mediation: Connects homeless high school students who are committed to graduating from high school with people willing to share their homes. Mediators facilitate rental contracts and a house rules discussion to provide a space for open communication and mutual expectation setting before move-in. Mediators check in with the parties after a few months to continue promoting open communication, contract modifications, and conflict resolution strategies as needed. Restorative Dialogue: Offers trained facilitators to help juvenile offenders and crime victims meet face-to-face. The dialogue is an opportunity to discuss the impacts of the harm, meaningful accountability, and make agreements to repair the harm. Takes most cases, with the exceptions of parenting plan, divorce cases, or court-ordered small claims, which are handled through Washington County. 12725 SW Millikan Way Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5:30pm Email (503) 526-2523 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 526-2523 General Questions Alternate (503) 526-3733 Housing Mediation Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call, email, or visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: City of Beaverton and Washington County east of 185th Avenue (Cities of Aloha, Durham, King City, Portland, Sherwood, Tigard, and Tualatin). Housing Mediation is available for all of Washington County. SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Services: Conflict Resolution Training Landlord/Tenant Dispute Resolution Mediation Mediation * Homeless Youth Mediation * Mobile Home Residents Mediation * People At Risk of Foreclosure Victim/Offender Mediation Programs * Juvenile Offenders Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - CITY OF BEAVERTON If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. CITY OF BEAVERTON
